title: Who is Brandon Holtz, golf's 'everyman' at the Masters?
description: Brandon Holtz, a 39-year-old real estate broker, is golf's 'everyman' at the Masters after winning the U.S. Mid-Amateur.
fullText:
Brandon Holtz is the proverbial Cinderella story out of nowhere at Augusta National Golf Club.

An unranked, 39-year-old reinstated amateur and Remax real estate broker from Bloomington, Illinois, Holtz won the U.S. Mid-Amateur in September at Troon Country Club in Arizona to punch his ticket into the field of 91 contestants at the 90th Masters.

Established in 1981 to provide a true national championship for post-collegiate amateurs 25 and older with a handicap of 2.4 or less, the U.S. Mid-Am attracted 5,942 entrants into the field. Holtz won his local qualifier and began the week as one of 264 qualifiers at 69 sites around the country who first had to endure 36 holes of stroke play on two courses with the low 64 advancing to a single-elimination match-play bracket.

Holtz played well enough to do so but nearly got bounced in his quarterfinal match. He trailed 3-down with five holes to go before he birdied all five to force extra holes and birdied the next to win. He proceeded to birdie the first three holes of his match in the semis for nine straight birdies over two matches.

“A lot of these guys were out here playing the golf course how it’s supposed to be played and I was just out there trying to make birdies,” he said. “I’m not a rocket science but in match play isn’t that what you’re supposed to do?”

In the final, the glass slipper fit. He benefited from good advice from Todd Mitchell, a friend from home who had made the Mid-Am final in 2008, who told him, “Stay in Arizona.” In other words, stay in the present, don’t begin thinking about what it would be like to play in the Masters. “He basically said, ‘Don’t make the same mistake I made,’ ” Holtz recalled.

He raced to a 5-up lead after 20 holes, but his opponent Jed Coughlin III trimmed his deficit to 1 down after 28 holes. A birdie at the 34th hole, and the Robert T. Jones Jr. Memorial Trophy and a gold medal belonged to Holtz, 3 and 2. In addition to the Masters invite, he earned a berth in this year’s U.S. Open, the next two U.S. Amateurs and is exempt into the next 10 U.S. Mid-Amateurs. After he won, he received 5,000 texts and emails, hearing from friends that he hadn’t heard from in years. It took nearly a month to respond to them all.

The Cinderella Story out of nowhere is 14 years older than any of the other five amateurs in the field and he has improved to 3,263 in the World Amateur Golf Ranking. The next highest ranked amateur in the field? No. 112. While Holtz concedes it’s surreal to be at the Masters, he’s quick to point out that he's no shrinking violet.

“There’s a reason we’re here,” Holtz said. “It’s not like we limped into it, although there probably are a lot of people that think that. I wouldn’t say I got lucky. I can play. If we went out and played, you’d find out.”

Holtz’s story is all the more remarkable considering he played college basketball – not golf – at Illinois State. He described his abilities as a three-point shooting specialist with self-deprecating humor: “I couldn’t run, I couldn’t jump, I couldn’t really dribble but I could shoot the hell out of it,” he said.

He turned pro as a golfer in 2010 and chased mini tours in the Carolinas, Florida, Georgia and Illinois, including the Hooters Tour, Peach State Tour and West Florida Golf Tour. He never won an event but only missed one cut. The largest check he ever cashed as a pro was for $14,000 for finishing runner up at the Illinois State Open. His biggest previous victory before winning the U.S. Mid-Am? A mini-tour event on the defunct National Pro Tour and more recently the City Medal Play in Bloomington after regaining his amateur status.

He beat around golf’s bush leagues for four years but when he got married and had two kids, it was time to find more steady work. Holtz previously sold sports equipment, but when COVID hit and youth sports shut down, he lost his job. Holtz is in his fourth year at ReMax, and it’s a job that allows him the flexibility to make his own schedule and squeeze in golf with his friends, who he said all are husbands and fathers like him. He applied to regain his amateur status in 2023, primarily so he could play with those buddies in local competitions, and was reinstated the following year.

Holtz has been to the Masters countless times thanks to his father, Jeff, who won lifetime badges in 2004. But they’ve never seen the Masters quite like this.

“We were always wondering what it’s like inside the ropes, what’s in that building and this year we found out a little bit in the practice rounds but we’ll get the entire show this week,” said Holtz, who had his dad on the bag when he won the U.S. Mid-Am and will have him reprise that role at the Masters.

They spent the maximum five days that competitors are allowed to spend at the club ahead of the Masters, and played 36 holes a day as well as bumping around the Par-3 course. He intends to enjoy all of the perks of being an amateur in the field, including spending at least one night in the Crow’s Nest. Fred Couples, a childhood favorite, and Gary Woodland, who played basketball in college, too, are high on his list to join for a practice round. The whole experience is the stuff of which golf dreams are made of, and golf’s everyman at the Masters is ready to give it his all.

“I’m a pretty realistic guy, the chances of me going out and winning the whole thing is a pretty low percent but I’m a competitor,” he said. “I want to make the cut and just want to compete for that low am.”

This article originally appeared on Golfweek: Brandon Holtz's 'everyman' journey to the Masters

title: Michigan record in championship games: How Wolverines can rewrite history with second national title
description: Michigan is looking to rewrite its history in the national championship game.
fullText:
Michigan record in championship games: How Wolverines can rewrite history with second national title originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Michigan has done plenty of winning on the national stage in its history, from the "Fab Five" to deep tournament runs in 2013 and 2018. Winning the final game has proven to be a monumental challenge.

The Wolverines have struggled in national championship games over the years, losing all but one in a major contract with their opponent this time around: the UConn Huskies.

UConn is 6-0 all-time in national title games, which sets the stage for a fascinating dynamic on Monday night because Michigan is such a decisive favorite.

Here's a look at Michigan's dismal record in championship games and how history can be rewritten against UConn.

MORE:Why Yaxel Lendeborg turned down more money from Kentucky

Michigan is just 1-6 in the national championship game in its history, including 0-4 over its last four appearances.

Michigan 80, Seton Hall 79 (OT)

Led by All-Americans Cazzie Russell and Bill Buntin, Michigan couldn't contain John Wooden's UCLA team, falling 91-80 in the championship game as Gail Goodrich erupted for 42 points. It was the second of nine titles in a span of 10 years for the Bruins.

1976 was a great year for Michigan, which narrowly won its first two NCAA Tournament games before making a run all the way to the national championship game, but that season will forever be known as Indiana's. The Hoosiers finished off an undefeated season with a blowout victory over the Wolverines in the title game.

Head coach Steve Fisher only took over Michigan at the start of the NCAA Tournament, but the surprise coaching change proved to be exactly what the Wolverines needed. Michigan rattled off six consecutive wins, with the Glen Rice-led team narrowly getting by Illinois and Seton Hall to capture the program's only national championship.

Michigan's only championship game victory came by a hair, as the Wolverines were taken to overtime by Seton Hall and trailed with three seconds remaining before Gerald Greene was controversially called for a foul that put Fisher's squad in position for the win.

The "Fab Five" beat Ohio State and Cincinnati to reach the national championship game, but Jalen Rose, Chris Webber and Juwan Howard were no match for Duke, scoring only 51 points in a blowout national championship game loss.

MORE:What to know about the 'Fab Five'

Michigan's iconic core got a chance at a redo in 1993 but came up short once again, falling 77-71 to North Carolina in the national championship game after taking down Rick Pitino and Kentucky in the national semifinal. The game is known for Chris Webber's timeout call with no timeouts remaining, which was a technical foul to help the Tar Heels seal a win.

Trey Burke was the hero all March long for No. 4 Michigan in 2013, single-handedly beating No. 1 Kansas in the Sweet 16 and powering the Wolverines to the Final Four. After taking care of business against Syracuse in the national semifinal, Louisville saw its title hopes slip away late against Pitino and Louisville in the championship game. The NCAA would later vacate the Cardinals' win, but that was no consolation to Michigan.

A miraculous shot from Jordan Poole revitalized Michigan in the second round, sparking a run to the Final Four, and the Wolverines earned another shot at a national championship after a second-half comeback against Loyola Chicago in the national semifinal. No one was better than Villanova, however, as Michigan found itself in a major hole early in the title game and could not recover in what can be called an uncompetitive game.

BENDER: Why Michigan will roll past UConn

Michigan hasn't won a national championship since 1989, which also marks the Wolverines' only title in program history.

It's been 37 years since Steve Fisher took over and led Michigan to a title in his first handful of games as head coach, despite numerous talented teams taking the floor for the Wolverines ever since. The "Fab Five" didn't win either of their two championship game appearances, while the 2013 team narrowly lost to Louisville and the 2018 team wasn't a match for Villanova.

Michigan looked like a contender again in 2021 but missed the Final Four as a No. 1 seed after being upset by No. 11 UCLA in the Elite Eight.

Michigan isn't the only Big Ten program with a surprising championship drought. In fact, the entire conference has struggled to win the big one.

The Big Ten's last national championship came all the way back in 2000, when Tom Izzo and Michigan State cemented their place in history. Despite title game appearances by Michigan, Purdue, Wisconsin, Michigan State, Illinois and Indiana in the years that followed, no one from the conference has finished the job. Michigan has yet another chance to change that.

MORE:Inside the Big Ten's championship game losing streak

title: Tennessee basketball freshman guard plans to transfer
description: Tennessee basketball freshman guard plans to transfer after the 2025-26 season.
fullText:
The 2025-26 college basketball season will conclude Monday with a national championship game between Michigan and UConn at Lucas Oil Stadium in Indianapolis, Indiana. Tipoff is slated for 8:50 p.m. EDT (TBS).

Tennessee advanced to its third consecutive NCAA Tournament Elite Eight in 2026.

The NCAA transfer portal will open Tuesday after the national title game.

Tennessee freshman guard Clarence Massamba plans to enter the NCAA transfer portal, according to Joe Tipton of On3.

"Tennessee guard Clarence Massamba plans to enter the NCAA transfer portal, Joe Tipton reports," On3 announced Monday.

Three players from Tennessee's 2025-2026 roster are graduating: point guard Ja'Kobi Gillespie, shooting guard Amaree Abram and center Felix Okpara. Tennessee has three commitments in its 2026 basketball recruiting class: small forward Ralph Scott, power forward Manny Green and point guard Marquis Clark.

Former Belmont shooting guard Tyler Lundblade committed to the Vols on April 1. Power forward Cade Phillips was the first member of Tennessee’s 2025-2026 basketball team to declare his entry into the transfer portal on Friday.

More: 2025 guard commits to Tennessee basketball

Follow Vols Wire on Facebook and X (formerly Twitter).

title: Chiefs predicted to trade for $37.9 million defensive star with No. 9 pick
description: This would be a bold move by Kansas City.
fullText:
Chiefs predicted to trade for $37.9 million defensive star with No. 9 pick originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

The Kansas City Chiefs find themselves in unfamiliar territory, holding the No. 9 overall pick in the 2026 NFL Draft.

If they don't like how it feels there, though, they could always make a trade instead of picking a player in that spot.

That's the idea suggested by ESPN's Bill Barnwell in a new article on Monday in a deal that would also include the Minnesota Vikings.

Chiefs get: 1-18, 3-97, Edge Jonathan Greenard

Vikings get: 1-9, Edge Felix Anudike-Uzomah

MORE: Eagles' new signing flew himself as a pilot to Philadelphia

Greenard would be the prize here.

"The Chiefs would love to come out of this draft with an impact edge rusher," Barnwell writes. "They might be just a tad too low to land that player at No. 9 if the board goes as expected. One other way to address that need would be to add a star in Greenard, who racked up 12 sacks and 22 knockdowns with the Vikings in 2024 before injuries wrecked his 2025 campaign. Greenard will turn 29 in May, so he should still have a couple of years of impactful pass rushing before the Chiefs would need to worry about moving on. Greenard has two years and $37.9 million remaining on his deal, and though Brett Veach & Co. would have to address that contract with a meaningful raise, Greenard could still be an easily justifiable addition for the Chiefs, given their perennial role as Super Bowl contenders."

Barnwell then writes that the Chiefs could use the 18th pick on a cornerback, with there likely not being a player to reach on at No. 9 at that position.

"For the Vikings, they move on from Greenard and potentially can draft a younger defensive impact player for DC Brian Flores with the ninth pick," Barnwell writes. "This trade would value the difference between Greenard and Anudike-Uzomah -- a former first-round pick who hasn't impressed with the Chiefs -- as being worth the 54th pick, according to the Johnson chart, in a typical draft. The Vikings would move up from No. 18 to No. 9 and put defensive coordinator Brian Flores in line to add one of the premier defensive backs in this year's class."

It'd be a headline-grabbing deal for both the teams involved and the fact that Greenard moves to the Chiefs. Maybe it's not too likely, but it's definitely intriguing.

title: Atlanta Braves News: Disappointing Split Against Diamondbacks, Drake Baldwin Goes Deep, More
description: A disappointing day in the desert.
fullText:
Through 10 games, the Braves are 6-4. While it is true anyone would take that start to the season, it also is a bit disappointing after the Braves had to settle for a series split vs the Dbacks. After a 6-5 loss in extra innings in another game where the Braves struggled with runners in scoring position, the Braves were unable to get a series win in a match-up where they clearly were the better team.

As they move on to face the Angels, Atlanta needs to get back on the right track on converting their run scoring opportunities.

Drake Baldwin went deep once again on Sunday, proving why he is the clear bright spot for the Braves to start the season.

The Dodgers already have 5 comeback wins this season, with the latest occurring on Sunday.

Mike Trout is Day to Day after taking a pitch off his hand Sunday. Notable news as the Braves face the Angels next.

Hunter Brown was placed on the IL with a shoulder injury.

Mookie Betts was placed on the IL with an oblique strain.

title: Fit-again Mbappe at Real Madrid for clashes like Bayern tie: Arbeloa
description: Real Madrid coach Alvaro Arbeloa said Kylian Mbappe joined the club to star in key matches like Tuesday's Champions League clash against German giants Bayern Munich."I'm delighted to have that kind of problem, to have outstanding players at my disposal and to have a player like Kylian Mbappe, who came to Real Madrid for matches like tomorrow's and for knockout ties like this one," Arbeloa told reporters Monday.
fullText:
Real Madrid coach Alvaro Arbeloa said Kylian Mbappe joined the club to star in key matches like Tuesday's Champions League clash against German giants Bayern Munich.

Arbeloa said Mbappe was a vital part of the team, although questions remain over whether record 15-time winners Madrid can strike the right balance with all their biggest names on the pitch at the same time.

French superstar Mbappe, the club's top goalscorer, did not start either game in the last 16 tie against Manchester City which Madrid won impressively, because of a long struggle with a knee sprain.

Fit once more, Arbeloa indicated Mbappe was likely to feature along with Vinicius Junior in attack at the Santiago Bernabeu against Bayern in the quarter-final first leg.

"I'm delighted to have that kind of problem, to have outstanding players at my disposal and to have a player like Kylian Mbappe, who came to Real Madrid for matches like tomorrow's and for knockout ties like this one," Arbeloa told reporters Monday.

"I'm sure we're going to see Kylian at his best, leading the team the way he's always known how to do, and I have a great deal of confidence in all the players."

England international Jude Bellingham is also finding his way back to fitness after injury and Arbeloa said he would keep betting on the squad's stars when they are available.

Against City young midfielder Thiago Pitarch and attacking midfielder Brahim Diaz played important roles, but they may get less time on the pitch in the Bayern tie.

"It's clear Mbappe has different characteristics and conditions to those Brahim has, so we have to play in a different way, but I'm delighted to have this problem," said Arbeloa.

"Just as you mention Bellingham and other great players I have available, for me it's a blessing to be able to count on everyone..." he continued.

"Having a player like Mbappe in the team is an extraordinary stroke of luck. I don't know if there's a coach in the world who wouldn't want to have him.

"I put myself in the shoes of the defenders who play against Real Madrid and have to face Mbappe, Vinicius, Valverde, Bellingham... I think they're some of the best players on the planet."

Brazilian winger Vinicius Junior said he worked well together with Mbappe, despite some believing the fit is not right for Madrid.

"People talk a lot... 'Kyli' is here to help us, he's always given us confidence, he scores the goals for us, we have to be connected tomorrow, above all with the fans and all the players," he told reporters.

"It's a difficult game where the best players make the difference, and Kyli is one of those. I have an incredible connection with him, not just on the pitch but off it too."

Vinicius pledged Madrid will be at their best against Bayern Munich after a poor showing in La Liga on Saturday, when they lost 2-1 against Mallorca, leaving them seven points adrift of leaders Barcelona.

"There are days where we are more connected or less connected, and that happened against Mallorca," explained the forward.

"We had come from the international break and today, as the coach said, if we're not at 200 percent, we're not going to win games.

"I hope we are tomorrow, because it's an important day for us."

The winger, who has become a leading figure in the fight against racism, also spoke about the Islamophobic chanting at Spain's friendly against Egypt last week, which Barcelona's teenage star Lamine Yamal criticised.

"It's important that Lamine speaks and that can help people," said Vinicius, who claimed he was racially abused by Benfica's Gianluca Prestianni in a Champions League match in February, which the Argentine denies.

"I'm not saying Spain, Germany or Portugal are racist countries, but there are racists in these countries, especially in Brazil too, in a lot of countries there are racists.

"But if we continue this fight together, I think that in the future new players don't have to suffer these things and above all, people in general too."

title: Opponent watch: Bayern Munich talisman trains normally ahead of Real Madrid clash
description: Bayern Munich enter the UEFA Champions League quarterfinal clash against Real Madrid with full steam, with the first leg set to be played at the Santiago Bernabeu tomorrow night.Ahead of the high-stak...
fullText:
Bayern Munich enter the UEFA Champions League quarterfinal clash against Real Madrid with full steam, with the first leg set to be played at the Santiago Bernabeu tomorrow night.

Ahead of the high-stakes clash, there was a doubt about the availability and participation of Bayern Munich’s star forward Harry Kane who appeared to be nursing an injury.

The English star did not play any part in his team’s comeback win at the weekend and all signs pointed to his participation against Real Madrid being in doubt. Recent days, however, saw optimism grow once more.

Now, as confirmed by Florian Plettenberg, Kane took part in Bayern’s first training session on Monday in Munich, handing the team a massive boost.

He was indeed dealing with an ankle injury, but he seems to have gotten past the setback. Barring any unusual development, he is expected to play against Real Madrid tomorrow.

Harry Kane has returned to training for Bayern Munich. (Photo by Adam Pretty/Getty Images)

It remains to be seen if the player is fit enough to start, but he will undoubtedly play some part and that, in itself, is enough of a worry for Real Madrid. After all, Kane has been in hot goalscoring form this season.

So far this season, the 32-year-old already has ten goals in the UEFA Champions League and a staggering 31 goals in the Bundesliga. He averages 4.2 shots and 1.2 goals per game in the domestic competition.

For Kane to be back for both legs of the game comes as a massive blow for Real Madrid, especially at a time when serious questions are being raised about the defensive department.

In order to get to the European title, however, Los Blancos must be prepared to get the better of the best in the business, and Kane fits that category.

title: 2026 NFL draft: Top 5 WRs, per Draft Wire
description: Draft Wire's Curt Popejoy has updated WR rankings for the NFL draft.
fullText:
The 2026 NFL draft is less than three weeks away, and the Arizona Cardinals could look at wide receivers. They already have Marvin Harrison Jr. and Michael Wilson, and they signed Kendrick Bourne in free agency, but everyone in the room after that is unproven in multiple seasons.

Who are the best receivers in the 2026 draft?

Draft Wire's Curt Popejoy named his top five receivers, although all five likely will be drafted in the first round.

All five could go in the first round. The Cardinals are not likely to select one in Round 1. But if one of them were to slip to the second round, that would be a real possibility.

title: Pirates have 'mountain to climb' to make play-offs
description: Cornish Pirates have "a mountain to climb" if they are to make the Champ play-offs, according to joint-head coach Gavin Cattle.
fullText:
Cornish Pirates have "a mountain to climb" if they are to make the Champ play-offs, according to joint-head coach Gavin Cattle.

The Pirates were beaten 36-12 at top-six rivals Bedford and dropped to seventh place - the top six make the play-offs.

The loss was Pirates' second in succession, after a defeat at home to Worcester Warriors last week that saw Cattle's fellow joint-head coach Alan Paver banned for six games after he was sent off for slapping an opposition player.

The Pirates' final four matches are all against sides in the top six.

"Let's face it, we've left ourselves a mountain to climb now," Cattle told BBC Radio Cornwall.

"It starts with Chinnor next week, Ealing away, Coventry at home, Hartpury away, so we've got to win three of those four to stand a chance, I think.

"So every game's a cup final for us now."

Pirates found themselves 24-5 down at half-time at Goldington Road after Fyn Brown, Lucas Titherington and Dean Adamson scored first-half tries for the hosts. Sol Moody's unconverted try was Pirates' only score.

Oisin Heffernan got a bonus-point try for Bedford 10 minutes into the second half before Alex Everett pulled one back in the final 10 minutes, only for Will Maisey to score a late fifth try for the home side.

"You've got to hand it to Bedford, physically there they were on top today, especially holding up in the tackle," added Cattle.

"It's something we'll have to look at next week, because if I was Chinnor I'd be licking my lips at that opportunity.

"Physically, we're better than that, but you've got to hand it to Bedford.

"I'm just disappointed that we didn't give a better showing of ourselves.

"We had a couple of errors in that first half. Whether that clouded our decision-making is something we'll have to look at."

title: 3 injured Arsenal stars return to training ahead of Sporting
description: Arsenal have received a timely boost ahead of the trip to Sporting, with three senior players back in training.Photo by James Fearn/Getty ImagesOne of the main talking points over the international br...
fullText:
One of the main talking points over the international break was the large number of Arsenal players who pulled out of international duty.

Arsenal fans are still waiting on a number of injury updates regarding key players but some who missed the FA Cup game against Southampton are now back in training.

On Saturday, Declan Rice, Bukayo Saka, Jurrien Timber, Eberechi Eze, Mikel Merino, Piero Hincapie, and Leandro Trossard were all missing from the squad.

Gabriel, who had a right knee problem with Brazil, started the game only to be taken off with a left knee issue.

He was replaced by William Saliba, whose own ankle problems meant he wasn’t quite right to start.

Noni Madueke, who picked up what looked like it could be a serious knee problem – he was in a brace at one point – made a surprise appearance in the squad, while Martin Odegaard also returned.

At Arsenal’s open training session before the travelled to Portugal, Rice and Trossard were spotted with the group, as was Gabriel, who seems to have already shaken off his problem.

The rest, however, did not seem to be involved, so it looks like Arsenal will be going without Timber, Eze, Merino, Hincapie, and Saka.

Of that list, the main concern is around Hincapie with Arteta admitting the injury could be ‘serious’.

title: Cowboys have path to trade for No. 2 overall NFL Draft pick from Jets
description: Dallas definitely needs some upgrades.
fullText:
Cowboys have path to trade for No. 2 overall NFL Draft pick from Jets originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

The Dallas Cowboys need to find defensive upgrades in the 2026 NFL Draft.

The good news is there are some potential superstars available on that side of the ball. The bad news is they may not make it to the Cowboys' spot at No. 12 overall.

That would point to a potential trade-up, and in a new article by ESPN's Bill Barnwell on Monday, he writes that the Cowboys could target the No. 2 overall pick, currently held by the New York Jets.

Barnwell would have the Cowboys send this year's 12th and 112th picks to the Jets, along with a 2027 first-round and a 2027 third-rounder. In return, Dallas would get the No. 2 overall pick and a 2027 fifth-rounder.

It's a steep price to pay, so the Cowboys would then need to get their choice right in the second spot.

"What if the Cowboys absolutely, positively love one of the defensive players in this draft and want to make sure they get that guy on their roster immediately?" Barnwell writes. "I know it's difficult to imagine Jerry Jones doing something rash and dramatic, but the Cowboys' owner has to fix Dallas' defense, preferably sooner rather than later. The Cowboys have essentially swapped out Osa Odighizuwa for Rashan Gary and added Jalen Thompson and Cobie Durant in the secondary, but even with Quinnen Williams in the mix, the Cowboys lack star power and difference-makers in their front seven."

MORE: Eagles' new signing flew himself as a pilot to Philadelphia

If a deal like this was coming together, the Cowboys would likely want to keep next year's first-rounder out of it, but the Jets would likely insist on it.

"He can get whoever he wants to make an immediate impact for the Cowboys at No. 2," Barnwell writes. "With this trade, the Cowboys also hold onto the 20th pick, which would allow them to address the secondary, either by selecting a player or moving down for extra draft capital. Suddenly, Jones can feel like his team is actually ... stacked on defense?"

The Jets would get another chance at adding their QB of the future next year (or trading up with all their accumulated assets to find him).

It might be a win-win trade, if the Cowboys pick the right guy after moving up.

title: Curling embraces WWE and darts in new professional league
description: Rock League, curling's first professional competition, aims to bring the energy of sports such as wrestling to the rink.
fullText:
Fans in costume, spectacular pyrotechnics and pounding walk-on music may not be commonly associated with the world of curling.

But a new professional competition for the sport is hoping to change its stuffy stereotype.

Rock League, which gets under way in Canada on Monday, aims to bring the energy of professional wrestling and electric atmosphere of darts to the rink.

Scottish curlers are among the 60 male and female athletes who will take part in the innovative format and hopes are high that the pilot project will lead to the league being fully rolled out next year.

The launch comes off the back of the Winter Olympics in Milan Cortina, which saw interest spike following controversial clashes in the men's and women's tournaments.

Team GB's men, entirely made up of Scottish athletes, took silver in the final while the mixed double team of Jennifer Dodds and Bruce Mouat reached the bronze medal match.

Will entertainment-led Rock League be future of curling?

World Curling embraces professional Rock League

Rock League founder, Nic Sulsky, said he hoped the competition would capitalise on a rise in popularity from the games.

However, he told the BBC's Radio Scotland Breakfast programme the "energy" off the rink was as important as the drama on it.

"One of the things that really drives sports fans to loving sports and loving the athletes is their desire to have fun," Sulsky said.

"But what has held curling down to a certain degree is that it has suffered a little from the stereotype of being quiet and being tame.

"A sport like darts, there's a real fun and energy where people show up in costumes.

"You look at other properties like wrestling and WWE is one that really puts a highlight on telling stories of who those incredible entertainers are, whether that is walk-ups and music.

"It's those types of things that help emphasise the excitement and the fun of the sport."

Five of the six franchises taking part in a pilot for the full competition at the historic Maple Leaf Gardens in Toronto from 6-12 April feature Scottish players.

Mouat will captain Northern United, where he will be joined by Dodds and former junior rink member Robin Brydone.

Olympic gold medallist Eve Muirhead will be general manager of the Alpine Curling Club, where Hammy McMillan Jr will be one of her players.

Meanwhile, Grant Hardie, Ross Whyte and Bobby Lammie will represent Frontier Curling Club, Maple United and Typhoon Curling Club respectively.

The winners will claim a share of a $250,000 (£189,000) prize pot.

Each team features five men's and five women's players.

Two teams will compete across three rinks in men's fours, women's fours and mixed pairs simultaneously.

However, the customary fourth rink will not go to waste and will instead host a VIP bar for fans to get as close as possible to the action.

McMillan told The Saturday Show some fans had already tried to bring the raucous atmosphere of Darts' World Championship at the Ally Pally to the rink.

Some have dressed as bananas to represent the "peel" – a shot designed to knock an opponents stone out of the way.

"Others celebrate the 'draw,' a shot where the rock is thrown to stay in play, by dressing up as crayons.

"We do really hope the Rock League kicks off," he said.

"The owners of the league see it a bit like darts, they are encouraging fans to dress up in costumes.

"I think we are going to see some cool, inventive things from the fans."

Curling is the latest sport to introduce a revamped, condensed format in a bid to attract a new generation of fans.

Cricket's The Hundred format, a limited overs variety of Twenty 20, has proved popular among supporters since it was launched in 2021.

Curling commentator and former player, Logan Gray, said it did have the potential to upset some of the sport's "traditional" fans.

But he said the prospect of maintaining interest outside of the "four-year Olympic cycle" could help clubs and rinks across country survive.

Gray told Radio Scotland Breakfast: "Every four years, there is curling on the telly.

"The public gets to know the players, they work the game out as it goes along.

"But it only happens every four years, you don't have the time every weekend to invest in that and go on that journey.

"This is something that is short, sharp, more exciting."

He added: "Across the world, there's facilities out there that are struggling, especially with utilities issues.

"We need to try something different to get new people in the door and to keep the sport affordable."

title: Max Verstappen hails Lionel Messi and highlights moment proving he is impossible to defend
description: Max Verstappen has praised Lionel Messi in a recent Red Bull Racing Instagram video, with the Formula 1 champion reflecting on the Argentine’s unique brilliance. Widely regarded as one of the greatest footballers ever, Messi continues to draw admiration across sports.
fullText:
Max Verstappen has praised Lionel Messi in a recent Red Bull Racing Instagram video, with the Formula 1 champion reflecting on the Argentine’s unique brilliance.

Widely regarded as one of the greatest footballers ever, Messi continues to draw admiration across sports. Verstappen, a multiple-time F1 world champion himself, is the latest example of it.

The Dutchman has won four consecutive F1 world championships, underlining his dominance. And his comments on the Inter Miami star highlight how generational talents recognise excellence beyond their own disciplines.

Speaking in a Red Bull Racing Instagram feature, Verstappen detailed why Messi’s qualities separate him from other players.

The four-time F1 champion said: “He [Messi] can read the game. You see a lot of people that, they need to run, they need to get in to score a goal. He can score from wherever, whenever. And you think you can defend him, but you cannot, you cannot stop him. So, he will score when you seize the opportunity.”

Verstappen pointed to a specific moment against the Netherlands at the 2022 World Cup, when Messi delivered a precise pass through a narrow gap to Nahuel Molina, leading to Argentina’s opener in a 2-2 draw.

According to him, that assist proves how it is impossible to stop the Argentine No.10.

“Like the ball he played against Holland at the last World Cup, it’s just… you cannot defend that,” he concluded.

Messi’s current performances suggest he remains ready for another World Cup, despite recent doubts about his involvement.

Argentina endured a difficult outing against Mauritania, but Messi responded by scoring and completing 90 minutes in a win over Zambia.

Back with Inter Miami, he also found the net in a 2-2 draw against Austin, reinforcing that his form remains strong heading into football’s biggest tournament.

title: Elina Svitolina climbs into Serena Williams’ ranking territory
description: Elina Svitolina hits ranking milestone matched only by Serena WilliamsSince returning to the WTA Tour following her pregnancy in 2023, Elina Svitolina has experienced a notable resurgence in her career. The Ukrainian has picked up three WTA titles since then, the most recent being the ASB Classic at the beginning of the 2026 season.
fullText:
Since returning to the WTA Tour following her pregnancy in 2023, Elina Svitolina has experienced a notable resurgence in her career.

The Ukrainian has picked up three WTA titles since then, the most recent being the ASB Classic at the beginning of the 2026 season.

Svitolina has made a strong start to 2026, reaching both a final and a semi-final at Masters events in Dubai and Indian Wells.

Her performances have also been reflected in her rankings climb. She now holds 25 consecutive weeks inside the top 10 of the WTA rankings as a mother.

This stretch matches a record set by Serena Williams for mothers on tour. The legendary American player held her top-10 spot for 25 consecutive weeks after becoming a parent as well.

Svitolina’s form hasn’t gone unnoticed, and there’s talk that she could push for another title before this year’s Wimbledon tournament rolls around.

Svitolina has climbed to world number seven in the rankings, following strong performances at the Sunshine Double.

That position makes her the highest-ranked mother on the WTA Tour since Williams reached the same mark three years ago.

Svitolina’s climb to seventh is a milestone she now shares with Williams, who also reached that ranking as a mum in 2021.

The pair now stand as the highest-ranked mothers in WTA singles since Kim Clijsters, who made it back to world number one after returning to tennis in 2011.

Next up for Svitolina is a trip to Stuttgart for the Women’s Open from 13-19 April, where Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff and Elena Rybakina are all set to feature among others from inside the top five.

title: Top college baseball freshmen to watch in 2026
description: Every college baseball season introduces a new wave of talent, but the 2026 freshman class feels particularly loaded with immediate-impact players and long-term stars. Across the country, from powerhouse SEC programs to rising contenders on the West Coast, first-year players…
fullText:
Every college baseball season introduces a new wave of talent, but the 2026 freshman class feels particularly loaded with immediate-impact players and long-term stars. Across the country, from powerhouse SEC programs to rising contenders on the West Coast, first-year players are not just filling roster spots—they are stepping directly into meaningful roles and reshaping expectations. This group blends advanced hitters with polished approaches, power arms with professional-level projection, and versatile athletes capable of contributing in multiple phases of the game.

What stands out most is how prepared many of these freshmen already look against high-level collegiate competition. Hitters like Angel Laya and Teddy Tokheim have demonstrated an ability to control at-bats and drive the baseball with authority, while players such as Jacob Parker and Nicholas Partida bring a level of completeness rarely seen this early in a college career. On the mound, arms like Cam Appenzeller and Sam Cozart showcase the kind of velocity and physicality typically associated with upperclassmen, hinting at future dominance as they refine their command.

Equally compelling is the depth of the class. Beyond the headline names, players like Michael Senay, Rett Johnson, and Caden Glauber offer diverse skill sets and high ceilings, suggesting that breakout performances could emerge throughout the season. Collectively, this freshman group reflects a broader trend in college baseball — young players arriving more polished, more confident, and more ready than ever before. As the 2026 season unfolds, these newcomers are poised not only to contribute but to define the trajectory of their programs.

Angel Laya has quickly emerged as one of the most polished freshman hitters in the country, already producing at an elite level in a major conference. His combination of physicality and barrel control allows him to impact the baseball with authority, reflected in his strong early slash line and power output. Evaluators have noted that he plays with the poise of a veteran, rarely looking overmatched against advanced pitching. Laya’s early inclusion on midseason All-American teams underscores how rapidly he has transitioned from prospect to centerpiece.

Teddy Tokheim has been one of the most explosive freshman bats in college baseball, blending plate discipline with high-end power production. His ability to drive the ball to all fields while maintaining a disciplined approach has made him a constant offensive threat in Stanford’s lineup. What separates Tokheim is not just production, but efficiency—he consistently turns quality at-bats into tangible results. His early statistical dominance places him among the most impactful freshmen in the nation.

Jacob Parker arrived on campus with significant pedigree and has validated that reputation with a steady, high-impact freshman campaign. Ranked among the top incoming players nationally, Parker has shown the ability to contribute both offensively and defensively at a high level. His bat-to-ball skills and projectable power give him long-term middle-of-the-order potential. Early All-America recognition suggests his ceiling is not just high—it’s already materializing.

Sam Cozart brings an imposing physical presence to the mound, and his raw tools suggest frontline potential for Texas. Standing 6-foot-6 with a power arm, he fits the archetype of a high-upside collegiate starter capable of overpowering hitters. As a freshman, his development hinges on command refinement, but the foundational traits—velocity, downhill plane, and durability—are already evident. Within a loaded Texas pitching staff, Cozart represents a long-term difference-maker.

Cam Appenzeller entered college baseball as one of the most highly regarded arms in his class, and expectations remain substantial. A hard-throwing left-hander with advanced feel for pitching, he combines velocity with a developing secondary arsenal. His ability to miss bats and handle high-leverage situations makes him a valuable asset even early in his career. Appenzeller’s trajectory points toward becoming a cornerstone arm in Tennessee’s rotation.

Rett Johnson profiles as a power arm with the type of raw stuff that translates well at the collegiate level. His fastball shows life through the zone, and his developing breaking pitches give him multiple avenues for generating swings and misses. As with many freshman pitchers, consistency will determine his immediate impact, but the ceiling is undeniable. Johnson’s progression will be closely monitored as he adjusts to higher-level competition.

Michael Senay is a versatile athlete whose value lies in both his defensive flexibility and offensive upside. Ranked among notable freshmen in his conference, he brings a dynamic skill set that allows him to contribute in multiple roles. His quick hands and athleticism suggest he can grow into a reliable offensive contributor as he gains experience. Senay’s adaptability should keep him on the field and accelerating his development curve.

Nicholas Partida entered the college ranks as a highly regarded recruit with strong offensive instincts. His advanced approach at the plate and ability to control the strike zone make him a difficult out, even against experienced pitching. Defensively, he offers stability and versatility, further increasing his value to Texas A&M. Partida’s well-rounded game gives him one of the higher floors among this freshman class.

Jett Kenady stands out for his defensive acumen behind the plate, a critical trait for any freshman catcher adjusting to the collegiate level. His receiving skills, arm strength, and game management ability allow him to earn early trust from pitching staffs. Offensively, he shows flashes of gap power with room to develop into a more consistent contributor. Kenady’s defensive foundation gives him a clear path to long-term impact.

Caden Glauber brings a polished repertoire to the mound, highlighted by his ability to mix pitches and disrupt timing. Unlike many freshmen arms, he relies less on raw velocity and more on command and pitch sequencing. That approach often translates well early in college careers, allowing him to compete immediately. As his physical tools continue to develop, Glauber could evolve into a highly dependable rotation piece.

Sawyer Pruitt offers intriguing two-way potential, combining offensive upside with defensive versatility. His swing is geared for consistent contact, but there is emerging power that could make him more than just a table-setter. On defense, his athleticism allows him to handle multiple positions effectively. Players with this kind of flexibility often become invaluable contributors over a long season.

Cole Hansen is a developmental arm with the kind of projection that coaches covet at the collegiate level. His frame and mechanics suggest there is still velocity to unlock, and his pitch mix provides a foundation for growth. Early in his career, his role may fluctuate, but his long-term outlook remains promising. Hansen represents the type of freshman who can make significant strides with structured development.

The 2026 freshman class represents a compelling mix of immediate production and long-term upside, with several players already performing like seasoned contributors. Their early impact underscores the evolving pipeline of talent entering college baseball, where readiness is no longer reserved for upperclassmen. As the season progresses, this group has the potential to shape both the national landscape and the future of the sport.

title: Mets Morning News for April 6, 2026
description: Your Monday morning dose of New York Mets and MLB news, notes, and links.
fullText:
If you’re assigning names to games this early in the season, you have yourself a little of the Jared Young game with his three hits and assist at second base, and a little of the Luis Torrens game as his pinch hit two-run double while hitting for Young gave the Mets a lead they would not give up in a 5-2 win over the Giants.

Choose your recap: Amazin’ Avenue, MLB.com, Newsday, New York Daily News, New York Post

Kodai Senga had a solid start in San Francisco and didn’t encounter another ligament-fizzling incident at first base, both of which are good signs for things going forward.

Brett Baty was scratched from Sunday’s lineup due to lingering effects of a jammed thumb, but no tests and no further concern are really necessary according to Carlos Mendoza.

The Washington Nationals had a 6-2 lead going into the top of the eighth inning, but were no match for a bunch of singles, doubles, and walks as by the time Edwin Diaz threw the last pitch, they had suffered an 8-6 loss.

In a four-run eighth inning more beneficial to a National League East team, the Marlins built a lead that they barely held onto, leaving New York with a 7-6 win in the finale against the Yankees.

Mickey Moniak haunted the team that once took him first overall in the draft as he slugged two homers in the Rockies’ 4-1 win over the Phillies.

Drake Baldwin connected for his fourth home run of the young season, but ultimately Ketel Marte walked the Braves off in the 10th inning of a 6-5 game.

The Dodgers completed their weekend sweep of the Nationals, but lost on a personnel level as Mookie Betts was sent to the injured list with an oblique strain and Hyeseong Kim was recalled in his place.

For the moment, the best baseball photograph of 2026 was taken by some fan with her iPhone from the seats at Angels Stadium.

Congratulations to Tony Vitello on his first ejection as a major league manager.

Mike Trout left Sunday’s game after taking a pitch to the back of his hand, though X-rays were negative and he will take a couple days off rather than some familiar, worse option.

Joey Luchessi’s time with the Los Angeles Angels big league squad barely crossed the 6-7 line as his DFA came after 9 total games.

The right knee injury that Jared Triolo suffered on Opening Day will keep him off the Pirates roster for at least a few weeks, but the team still expects the lion’s share of the season to have him healthy and on the field.

For the first time in his career, Hunter Brown is going on the injured list for what is currently an unknown amount of time with a shoulder strain.

On this date in 2004, Kazuo Matsui peaked on the first pitch he saw and hit a home run.

title: Anonymous NFL coach shares why an 18-game season could be disastrous
description: An expanded season is finding growing support among owners, but it's not universally supported.
fullText:
In 2021, the NFL expanded to 17 games, marking it's first real schedule expansion in over 40 years. Since then, there's been lots of speculation that the league wants to eventually move to 18 games, and that talk has been heating up recently.

At the NFL owners meeting, it seemed that the push for another game might be getting more likely to occur, as some owners came out in support of the league potentially making the move. But it's becoming clear that the support for moving to 18 games might not be as widespread as the league would like it to be.

"Eighteen games will water down the product," said one NFL coach and former player. "What made the NFL different from the rest of the competition is every game matters. You had 16 intense weeks, and there’s nothing like it.

"Now we have 17 games, and with one extra game some players are looking to take a game off. Eighteen games will definitely get players thinking about taking games off. It will only work if the players get rewarded handsomely. They will sell their souls for money."

One NFL agent echoed these statements, arguing that he thinks the schedule is fine the way it is, and that most players are not actually going to want the league expand the schedule by one more game. He believes the league will have to meet the players in the middle on the matter.

"I think there are enough regular-season games," an NFL agent told me. "The league will need to pay a massive price to get the players to do it. Most players who actually play would not be in favor of it. Most can’t make it through 17 games. The league has zero ability to do it unilaterally."

This article originally appeared on Touchdown Wire: Anonymous NFL coach shares why an 18-game season could be disastrous

title: Man City confirm Bernardo Silva exit but can Juventus afford him?
description: Manchester City stalwart Bernardo Silva is on his way out of the club at the end of the season, with Juventus keen to recruit his services.The 31-year-old has been a pillar in Pep Guardiola’s team s...
fullText:
Manchester City stalwart Bernardo Silva is on his way out of the club at the end of the season, with Juventus keen to recruit his services.

The 31-year-old has been a pillar in Pep Guardiola’s team since making the move from Monaco in the summer of 2017.

The attacking midfielder has gone on to make 450 appearances for the Cityzens, winning a host of trophies, including six Premier League titles, and the 2022/23 edition of the Champions League.

With his contract expiring at the end of the season, Silva’s departure has been in the air for quite some time.

But following Man City’s FA Cup win over Liverpool on Saturday, Guardiola’s assistant coach, Pep Lijnders, has all but confirmed the news.

“Every good story comes to an end,” said the Dutchman following his club’s 4-0 hammering of the Reds.

“I hope Bernardo enjoys his last six weeks, that he has a good ending because he deserves it. And he deserves all the attention he can get.”

Following this announcement, the race for the Portuguese star’s services is expected to heat up in the coming days, with Juventus taking part, having monitored the player for months.

According to Tuttosport, April could be decisive, with Silva potentially making a decision by the end of the month.

The Turin-based newspaper confirms that Juventus had already made exploratory contacts with the player’s agent, Jorge Mendes.

Silva reportedly has rich suitors in Major League Soccer and the Saudi Pro League, but he prefers to embark on one final experience at a top European club.

The Bianconeri believe the 31-year-old can be a game-changer for Luciano Spalletti, providing the missing quality in the middle of the park.

However, the player expects a net salary of at least €8m-€9m, while Juventus have recently set their wage ceiling at €7 million.

Therefore, the Serie A giants might be forced to make an exception for the Man City skipper to secure the deal.

title: We asked, you answered: fans predict Mike Green breakout in 2026
description: Check out the results from last week’s Ravens Reacts survey!
fullText:
In last week’s Ravens Reacts survey, 61% of Baltimore Beatdown readers predicted that outside linebacker Mike Green would break out this season.

Despite a pre-draft billing as an explosive pass rusher, the second-round pick disappointed as a rookie. Injuries to Kyle Van Noy and Tavius Robinson – as well as the Odafe Oweh trade – pressed Green into a bigger role than expected. He led the outside linebacker room in snaps, but only produced 3.5 sacks and seven tackles for loss. The 22-year-old struggled to set a hard edge against the run or consistently generate pressure on opposing quarterbacks.

Green stands to benefit from Baltimore’s new defensive coaching staff. One of the drawbacks of Zach Orr’s scheme was a prescribed rush plan that handed edge rushers specific responsibilities rather than letting them pin their ears back. Jesse Minter’s scheme allows them to play freer, as evidenced by Odafe Oweh’s production after being traded to the Chargers last season. He notched 7.5 sacks in 12 games in Los Angelez after zero to start the season in Baltimore. Minter could similarly unlock Green, both by allowing him to trust his athleticism and instincts as a straight-up rusher and using him as a looper on blitzes.

The addition of Trey Hendrickson should also create more opportunities for Green. With opposing teams more focused on his more proven teammate, he should draw more 1-on-1 matchups without tight end help, which will allow him to get upfield and turn the corner against slower tackles.

The Ravens may still prioritize the edge rusher position in April’s draft, but a second-year breakout from Green could have a bigger impact. Baltimore would finally have the kind of dangerous edge rushing duo they have lacked for a decade, turning arguably the most deficient area of their defense into a game-changing strength.

title: Championship Monday Thoughts And UNC’s Search Continues
description: Monday night is going to be interesting and so is UNC’s search.
fullText:
The national championship is Monday night, and things have changed a bit since our first look at the matchup between Michigan and UConn.

For the Wolverines, Yaxel Lendeborg, who injured his ankle and knee, is expected to play. And UConn’s Solo Ball has been in a boot since UConn’s win over the Illini.

It’s hard to know how that will play out, but UConn’s margin of error is probably thinner.

Both are expected to play, and there’s no point in holding anything back, because no one is playing after Monday night.

Michigan spread their minutes around more than UConn Saturday. Michigan had to play more guys because Lendeborg got hurt and only played 14 minutes, but otherwise, the starters went for 22, 25, 30, and 32 minutes respectively, and had nine players in double digit minutes.

Will Tschetter got 15 minutes, mostly in relief of Lendeborg, while Trey McKenney got 27 minutes, and Roddy Gale got 30.

Elliot Cadeau finished with 4 fouls, while Morez Johnson and Alda Mara got 3 each.

For the Huskies, the starters played 35, 35, 31, 30, and 28 minutes respectively, with seven players in double digit minutes. Silas Demary picked up four fouls, and Malachi Smith got four off the bench.

Tarris Reed, Braylon Mullins, and Eric Reibe got three each. Smith burned four in 9 minutes, while Reibe got his in just 5 minutes.

That, along with Solo’s possible limitations, makes UConn’s margin for error much less than it appeared Saturday evening. 
However, balance that against this: Danny Hurley has proven to be a superb tournament coach, and Michigan has to control Reed, who has proven to be a real handful.

Speaking of Michigan, Dusty May has made it clear that he won’t take the UNC job. That means that May, Arizona’s Tommy Lloyd, Iowa State’s TJ Otzelberger, Vanderbilt’s Mark Byington, and Boston Celtics GM Brad Stevens have all either explicitly said no or negotiated a better contract.

The last guy on the elite list of coaches UNC targeted is Chicago Bulls coach Billy Donovan, and he’s made it clear that he won’t discuss the job seriously until his season ends on April 12th.

Byington might still bite, but his buyout has gone up substantially.

So who else is potentially on the list?

Well, two names have come up: Baylor’s Scott Drew and Texas Tech’s Grant McCasland.

Drew is a great fit in many ways, but his success has really trailed off since winning the national championship in 2020-21. That team won 28 games. From there, Baylor has won 27, 23, 24, 20, and this year finished 17-17.

Drew may have seen better days.

As for McCasland, he’s a terrific young coach. Alas, he, too, signed an extension, which was announced late Sunday evening. His deal extends him for six more seasons and is worth $31.5 million. The buyout is now $10 million.

So it may be time to aim a bit lower, and that’s alright. The ACC has always excelled at finding great young coaches on the rise. UNC hired Dean Smith when he was an assistant. Mike Krzyzewski was at Army; Jim Valvano was at Iona. Bobby Cremins was at Appalachian State, and Lefty Driesell and Terry Holland were both at Davidson before moving to Maryland and Virginia respectively.

So who is available who is young and promising?

Siddle and Clayman are both nearby. Siddle grew up in Eden and knows the historical ACC as only a native of the region could. He’s been quite successful at Wilmington, averaging 25.2 wins over the last five years and 27 in three of those, including the last two. That’s much harder to do at a school like Wilmington than it is at a bigger platform.

Clayman would be a risky hire, but if you weren’t impressed by High Point, you weren’t paying attention. That team was beautiful. Do you think UNC fans could pull for a team that played like that one did?

Hell, yes. Bonus: both are quite young, with Siddle, 39, and Clayman, 37.

Risky hires? Yes. Big risk, and potentially huge rewards.

Savage has been less successful at ETSU, but is well-regarded, and Young is widely expected to be. head coach soon. Bonus for Savage: his kids are named Waverly and Sloane. They’ll fit right in in Chapel Hill.

Siddle is probably the safest bet of these, but UNC will eat buffalo chips before it hires a coach from UNCW.

As for Moton, he has done a tremendous job at Central and might be hugely successful in Chapel Hill, given the increase in resources that he’d have.

We’d like to propose a more radical solution, one that we think most UNC fans could embrace or, at least, live with. However, it would take some tweaking to make this work.

Yes, he’s young (just 32) and untested, but he’s well-regarded by his colleagues in the profession.

But his youth could work in his favor when it comes to persuading players to come to Chapel Hill, whether high school talents, or from Europe, where he played professionally, or from the transfer portal.

Hiring the right staff would be key, but the Family could help here. We could imagine hiring guys like Jerry Stackhouse and Rasheed Wallace, in some capacity. Stackhouse was average at Vandy, but he knows the game and the UNC system, and his NBA reputation would help. As for Wallace, he has a brilliant mind for basketball that gets obscured by his hot-headed nature and his sometimes goofy demeanor. But that guy’s basketball IQ is very, very high

You could flesh out the staff with various other former Tar Heels if you chose, which would buy you some patience from the fans. Would Michael Jordan be willing to play some sort of a role? Possibly, if it meant keeping the Family going.

For Paige to succeed, you’d need two things most of all: first, a gifted and relentless GM. Second, an older coach who would serve as a consigliere of sorts. You know, a guy who is out of coaching, but who had some success. He wouldn’t need to spend a ton of time in the gym. Most of his work would be just getting Paige up to speed. In fact, it might be a 1-2 year part-time job, a chance for a guy to just dip his toe in a bit.

Ideally, it would be a local guy, who maybe even goes to the games a lot, and who really wants UNC to succeed.

Fortunately, there is a guy who fits this description precisely: Ol’ Roy Williams.

He’s never coaching again, but he knows the program, presumably holds Paige in high regard, and would have a chance to rebuild the Family in a way that could take it 20-30 years into the future.

Obviously, the risk would be high, but if it worked?

The reward could be staggering.

If Williams isn’t interested, maybe Stackhouse would be. But if not, here’s another guy who could play that role: former Kansas State coach Jerome Tang.

Yes, he flamed out at K-State, but Baylor’s decline started when he left for the Wildcats. As a coaching consigliere, he could help Paige get up and running and teach him things he must learn.

As you might have guessed, we think highly of Paige. Structure his early UNC staff well, and that guy might knock it out of the park.

title: Rival watch: Atletico Madrid to welcome three stars back ahead of Barcelona game
description: Barcelona and Atletico Madrid are already preparing for their next match-up which is scheduled days after their meeting in La Liga that ended in a win for the Catalans.The next meeting will take place...
fullText:
Barcelona and Atletico Madrid are already preparing for their next match-up which is scheduled days after their meeting in La Liga that ended in a win for the Catalans.

The next meeting will take place at the Spotify Camp Nou and will be a high-stakes night for the Blaugrana as they will look to secure a lead on aggregate in the first-leg of the UEFA Champions League quarterfinal.

Still feeling the fatigue of a high-intensity game at the weekend, Barcelona will not have it easy on the night, especially with even Marc Bernal being injured. Atletico Madrid, however, will appear more rested and with a fuller squad.

Diego Simeone managed to hand several key starters a rest at the weekend, and will not mind the defeat much given his team’s situation in La Liga. However, he is also set to gain some players back in his squad, as relayed by AS.

Jan Oblak is back for Atletico (Photo by Juan Manuel Serrano Arce/Getty Images)

For starters, Jan Oblak trained with the group earlier today, which comes as a massive boost for the Colchoneros in goal. He will likely replace Juan Musso in the starting lineup.

Marc Pubill is another player who returned to group training and is thus expected to make it for the first leg against Barcelona. He has not played since mid-March and may well return to the starting lineup.

A third player who will be back in Simeone’s plans is Rodrigo Mendoza, who may not start but will serve as a valuable option off the bench regardless.

Johnny Cardoso and Pablo Barrios continued to train away from the club and are expected to be unavailable for the game midweek.

Barcelona, meanwhile, will be without four players as Marc Bernal became the latest name to join Raphinha, Frenkie de Jong, and Andreas Christensen on the sidelines.

title: NBA MVP race 2026, ranking the leading candidates
description: Every season, the MVP conversation starts somewhere around November, simmers through the winter, and by March, it’s a full boil. This year? It never really cooled down. The 2025-26 NBA MVP race is as wide open and fiercely contested as…
fullText:
Every season, the MVP conversation starts somewhere around November, simmers through the winter, and by March, it’s a full boil. This year? It never really cooled down. The 2025-26 NBA MVP race is as wide open and fiercely contested as any the league has seen in recent memory, and the reason is simple: there is no one dominant, runaway candidate. Instead, you have a handful of players each making a compelling case in a different way, each pulling voters in a different direction.

One guy is piling up historic scoring numbers every single night. Another is doing things statistically that no center in history has ever done. A third is anchoring the league’s best team and doing it with surgical efficiency. A fourth was traded to the Lakers and immediately reminded everyone why he’s one of the most gifted scorers and playmakers alive. And a fifth started the season as hot as anyone in the league before injury derailed his momentum.

MORE: NBA players with the best nicknames

That last part matters because the MVP award has always been as much about narrative as it has about numbers. Voters weigh team success, individual dominance, availability, and impact. This season, all of those factors cut differently for each candidate, and no one ticks every box perfectly. That’s what makes this race so compelling and so genuinely hard to call.

Five players have separated themselves from the field. Here they are, ranked from the outer edge of the conversation to the man who, right now, has the strongest case.

Season stats: 27.6 PPG | 9.8 RPG | 5.4 APG

Giannis was the most dangerous player in the NBA for the first few weeks of this season, looking every bit like a man on a mission to win his third MVP. He was scoring in bunches, dominating the boards, and carrying Milwaukee with the kind of force that made the Bucks look like a legitimate contender. Then the injuries came, first a groin strain, then a calf issue, and the missed games piled up at exactly the wrong time. His numbers are still elite when he’s on the floor, but availability is part of the award, and Giannis simply hasn’t been available enough to make a stronger case.

Season stats: 33.5 PPG | 7.7 RPG | 8.3 APG

Luka’s return to the spotlight this season has been vintage Dončić, a relentless volume of points, dimes, and clutch moments that remind you why he’s one of the most naturally gifted offensive players the league has produced in a generation. He moved up to No. 2 on the official MVP Ladder in late March after a scoring barrage that turned heads across the league, and the Lakers have given him the stage to operate. The question voters keep coming back to is team record, and that’s the one area where Luka’s case gets complicated. But no one in this race is more dangerous to defend on any given night.

Season stats: 27.9 PPG | 12.9 RPG | 10.9 APG | 67.2 TS%

This is now a recurring conversation: how do you not give Jokic the MVP when he’s averaging a near triple-double and leading this entire field in assists, rebounds, and true shooting percentage? The Joker is doing things no character in the history of the game has ever done with this consistency. The knock on him this year is team success: Denver sits fourth in the West at 50-28, and in a field this competitive, that matters. But the statistical case Jokic builds every single night remains one of the most absurd things happening in professional basketball.

Season stats: 24.9 PPG | 11.6 RPG | 3.1 APG | 3.1 BPG | +10.7 plus/minus

Wembanyama’s MVP case is unlike anyone else’s in this race. His scoring numbers are the lowest of the top candidates, but no one changes a game the way he does on both ends of the floor. The 3.1 blocks per game tell only part of the story; his mere presence warps how opposing offenses are designed. San Antonio is second in the entire NBA at 59-19, and that rise has added enormous weight to his candidacy. Wembanyama surged to the top of the official NBA MVP Ladder at the end of March, and it’s not hard to see why.

Season stats: 31.6 PPG | 4.4 RPG | 6.5 APG | 66.4 TS% | +11.3 plus/minus | Team record: 62-16 (1st in NBA)

SGA’s MVP case is built on the cleanest combination of individual excellence and team success in this entire race. He is putting up elite scoring on elite efficiency, his plus/minus leads the entire field by a significant margin, and the Oklahoma City Thunder are the best team in basketball. He has scored 30 or more points in game after game, done it without leaning on volume shooting, and made the reigning MVP award look like it belongs squarely on his mantle. When voters weigh everything, the Thunder’s record combined with SGA’s numbers makes a case that is genuinely hard to beat.

The MVP race still has a few weeks left to breathe, and anything can happen between now and the final ballot. But right now, the evidence points to one name more than any other. SGA has the numbers, the efficiency, and the wins. Wembanyama has the two-way dominance, and a surging Spurs team right behind him. And Jokic, as always, is sitting quietly in the background with a stat line that defies logic. However this ends, the 2026 MVP race will be talked about for a long time.

title: Broncos' order of picks in 2026 NFL draft
description: Take a look at the Broncos' order of picks in the 2026 NFL draft.
fullText:
After acquiring wide receiver Jaylen Waddle in a trade with the Miami Dolphins last month, the Denver Broncos now hold seven picks in the 2026 NFL draft.

The trade came at a high cost as Denver agreed to send picks in the first (No. 30), third (No. 94) and fourth (No. 130) rounds to Miami in exchange for Waddle and a fourth-round pick (No. 111). After that, the Broncos dropped from holding nine picks down to seven, and they will not make a pick in the first round (barring another trade).

Denver's first pick is currently slated to be 62nd overall in the second round. The Broncos do not hold a third-round pick, but with a pair of fourth-round selections, that could make it easier to trade up. Here's a look at the team's complete order of picks ahead of the draft.

Round 4: 108th overall (via Saints; Devaughn Vele trade)

Round 4: 111th overall (via Dolphins; Jaylen Waddle trade)

Round 7: 256th overall (Javonte Williams comp pick)

Round 7: 257th overall (Riley Dixon comp pick)

The extra fourth-round pick was acquired from the New Orleans Saints in exchange for wide receiver Devaughn Vele last year. Denver also received a pair of seventh-round compensatory picks after losing running back Javonte Williams and punter Riley Dixon during free agency last spring.

The Broncos lost their original sixth-round pick when the team acquired defensive lineman John Franklin-Myers in a trade with the New York Jets in 2024. After letting JFM walk in free agency this year, Denver will likely receive a fourth-round comp pick for next year's draft.

The 2026 NFL draft will be held in Pittsburgh from April 23-25.

Social: Follow Broncos Wire on Facebook and Twitter/X! Did you know: These 25 celebrities are Broncos fans.

This article originally appeared on Broncos Wire: Denver Broncos: Order of picks in 2026 NFL draft

title: Can Mikel Arteta take the last step to win silverware?
description: If Mikel Arteta left North London tomorrow, he could claim he had left Arsenal in a healthier position than when he arrived.Our manager continues to adapt his tactics to find a way to compete with Pep...
fullText:
If Mikel Arteta left North London tomorrow, he could claim he had left Arsenal in a healthier position than when he arrived.

Our manager continues to adapt his tactics to find a way to compete with Pep Guardiola.

The final step for the Spaniard to prove is that he can get over the line.

That is what separates the good from the great.

That is why it is premature for any Gooner to suggest our manager would be lifting a quadruple this season.

Simply because that is not where our club is in its development.

Manchester City currently have the advantage because they possess core members of their squad who understand not only how to perform under pressure but also how to do so with confidence.

That is not a criticism. The final step is meant to be the most difficult. Many can reach for the brass ring, but if it were easy to grasp, we would not be enduring a 22-year title drought.

In the next month, Arteta will earn his salary.

In the early years of his coaching career, he might have needed fresh ideas, but now he and his staff must find a better way to ensure a group of talented individuals embraces the coming weeks rather than fear them.

It has been observed that for much of the campaign, the manager has been calmer on the touchline and more composed in interviews.

His track record suggests, however, that the bigger the occasion, the more his players have struggled to deliver.

I recently watched a documentary explaining how Atlético Madrid needed to avoid defeat at Barcelona to win La Liga.

Despite trailing 1-0 at halftime, players later revealed that at the interval, Diego Simeone convinced them they were exactly where they wanted to be.

There are no guarantees that Arteta can replicate that approach, as not every manager possesses that ability.

The first time a team crosses the finish line, the next time often becomes easier.

Arteta now needs to demonstrate that he can halt any momentum against his side.

That is what defines the very best.

_____________________________________________________________________________________________

So here are some simple rules which I must insist commenters follow….

You agree not to give any personal abuse to other Arsenal fans. Everyone is allowed to hold their own opinions even if you disagree with them. It COSTS NOTHING TO BE POLITE TO OTHER ARSENAL FANS.
